Title: The Arabidopsis APOLO and human UPAT sequence-unrelated long noncoding RNAs can modulate DNA and histone methylation machineries in plants

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes thermomorphogenesis-associated hypocotyl elongation under warm temperature
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: APOLO modulates auxin-related thermomorphogenesis by repressing YUC2 at 23°C and, when deregulated, impairing YUC2 heat induction and hypocotyl elongation; APOLO overexpression also decoys VIM1/LHP1 from chromatin and lowers YUC2 expression
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIRP-MS; ChIRP-dot blot; RIP; ChIRP-qPCR; DRIP-qPCR; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; hypocotyl phenotyping; ChIP-qPCR; MeDIP-qPCR; CRISPR deletion; RNAi and overexpression analyses
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. APOLO physically associates with VIM1 and previously known partner LHP1. 2. APOLO directly recognizes the YUC2 locus through sequence complementarity/R-loop formation. 3. APOLO levels are reduced at 29°C, and deregulation of APOLO impairs heat-induced YUC2 activation and hypocotyl elongation. 4. APOLO overaccumulation reduces VIM1 and LHP1 binding at YUC2 promoter and alters DNA methylation/H3K27me3-related regulation.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Decreases after 6 h at 29°C compared with 23°C in seedlings; previously cold-induced per discussion
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Warm temperature treatment at 29°C; standard condition 23°C
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Both overexpression and depletion can impair proper thermomorphogenic growth, indicating dosage sensitivity
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential epigenetic engineering lever for temperature-responsive auxin traits, but strong dosage/stoichiometric effects caution against constitutive misexpression
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Coordinates DNA methylation and H3K27me3 machinery at YUC2 via interaction with VIM1 and LHP1; forms R-loops at YUC2
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Sequence-unrelated to UPAT but functionally analogous in decoying VIM1/LHP1 from chromatin

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes thermomorphogenesis-associated hypocotyl elongation via YUC2-dependent auxin biosynthesis context
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Constitutive UPAT expression in Arabidopsis decreases YUC2 transcript accumulation and impairs VIM1/LHP1 binding to YUC2 promoter, mimicking APOLO overexpression-associated transcriptomic effects
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient RIP in plant cells; in vitro RNA-protein interaction assay; stable overexpression in Arabidopsis; ChIP-qPCR; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Human lncRNA UPAT interacts with plant VIM1 and LHP1 in planta despite lacking sequence homology with APOLO. 2. UPAT overexpression reduces VIM1/LHP1 occupancy at YUC2 promoter. 3. UPAT constitutive expression decreases YUC2 transcript levels and globally alters the Arabidopsis transcriptome similarly to APOLO overexpression.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed in transgenic Arabidopsis lines used in this study
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Primarily standard 23°C conditions; also interpreted in thermomorphogenesis context
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Heterologous constitutive expression perturbs endogenous transcriptome and YUC2 regulation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Conceptual tool showing cross-kingdom lncRNA structural mimicry, but not a direct crop target
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Acts as a decoy for VIM1/LHP1 chromatin binding in plants
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Source species is human but metabolic effect assessed in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes warm-temperature hypocotyl elongation through enabling YUC2 activation and auxin-related thermomorphogenesis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: VIM1 positively regulates YUC2 heat responsiveness and thermomorphogenesis; vim1 mutants show reduced YUC2 induction and hypocotyl elongation, with altered DNA methylation at YUC2 promoter
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIRP-MS; ChIRP-dot blot; RIP; ChIP-qPCR; MeDIP-qPCR; mutant and complementation analyses; RNA-seq; BiFC; subcellular localization
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VIM1 is an APOLO-interacting methylcytosine-binding protein. 2. VIM1 expression is induced by warmth, and vim1 mutants are defective in warm-temperature hypocotyl elongation. 3. VIM1 binds the YUC2 promoter, with occupancy reduced at 29°C. 4. VIM1 cooperates with LHP1 to regulate DNA methylation and H3K27me3 at YUC2.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced after 6 h at 29°C in seedlings
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Methylcytosine-binding protein; UHRF1 homolog; contains PHD, SRA, RING, spacer domains
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: APOLO; LHP1; VIM2 (APOLO also binds VIM2); likely MET1 per known function discussed
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation impairs thermomorphogenic growth; overexpression gives only minor enhancement, suggesting context dependence
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential epigenetic regulator target for tuning temperature-responsive growth, but pleiotropic methylation effects likely
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Controls DNA methylation at YUC2 promoter and is required for proper LHP1/H3K27me3 behavior
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not an enzyme or TF, VIM1 is a core epigenetic regulator and thus classified here as Common Gene

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes proper thermomorphogenic hypocotyl elongation through regulation of YUC2-associated chromatin state
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: LHP1 is required for YUC2 heat induction and normal hypocotyl elongation at warm temperature; lhp1 mutants show reduced DNA methylation changes and no YUC2 activation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-seq reference; ChIP-qPCR; mutant phenotyping; RT-qPCR; BiFC; RIP with UPAT
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LHP1 binds the YUC2 locus and its occupancy/H3K27me3 deposition decline at 29°C. 2. lhp1 mutants fail to activate YUC2 properly and have impaired warm-temperature hypocotyl elongation. 3. LHP1 physically interacts with VIM1 and binds APOLO/UPAT-related complexes. 4. APOLO or UPAT overexpression decoys LHP1 away from YUC2 chromatin.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: LIKE HETEROCHROMATIN PROTEIN 1; PRC1-associated chromatin protein
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq context via associated study datasets (GEO: GSE167879, GSE210828)
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely pleiotropic chromatin effects beyond auxin pathway
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for epigenetic control of temperature-responsive growth, but broad Polycomb-related pleiotropy is a major caution
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Mediates H3K27me3 deposition and cooperates with VIM1-dependent DNA methylation at YUC2
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Regulatory role is chromatin-based rather than transcription-factor-based, so classified as Common Gene

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: YUCCA flavin monooxygenase step of indole-3-pyruvic acid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Indole-3-acetic acid/auxin (functionally inferred from YUCCA family role in auxin biosynthesis)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the oxidative conversion in the YUCCA auxin biosynthetic step leading from indole-3-pyruvic acid to indole-3-acetic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Hypocotyl tissues in seedlings under warm temperature response context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: YUC2 is heat-induced in WT seedlings and required for proper warm-temperature hypocotyl elongation; its activation is impaired in APOLO-, VIM1-, or LHP1-deregulated backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: proYUC2:GUS expression assay; loss-of-function mutant phenotyping; RT-qPCR; ChIRP-qPCR; DRIP-qPCR; ChIP-qPCR; MeDIP-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. YUC2 is a direct chromatin target co-regulated by APOLO, VIM1, and LHP1. 2. Its promoter is activated in hypocotyls at 29°C and yuc2 mutants show reduced hypocotyl elongation. 3. YUC2 induction by heat requires proper APOLO/VIM1/LHP1-dependent epigenetic remodeling, including DNA methylation and H3K27me3 changes. 4. Overexpression of APOLO or UPAT lowers YUC2 expression by impairing VIM1/LHP1 binding.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by 29°C in 4-day-old seedlings, notably in hypocotyl context
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: proYUC2:GUS native promoter reporter; 35S-CaMV used for upstream regulator constructs in some assays
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: APOLO-associated chromatin; VIM1; LHP1; Pol V/RdDM-associated factors inferred at promoter
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step in thermomorphogenesis-associated auxin biosynthesis, as supported by mutant phenotype and direct regulation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered YUC2 regulation affects growth architecture under warm conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering temperature-responsive growth/auxin output, but should consider epigenetic context and broader auxin homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Promoter and gene body are controlled by APOLO-mediated R-loops, VIM1-dependent DNA methylation, LHP1/H3K27me3, and likely RdDM
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper focuses on epigenetic control of YUC2 rather than biochemical enzyme assay
